contextBlurb: South Dakota has taken notable action to protect residents from data center strain on the grid, with SB135 and HB1038 both enacted to give regulators authority over data center energy costs and allow utilities to pass actual infrastructure costs back to large customers. Beyond those enacted measures, lawmakers are actively debating a broad range of additional proposals, including a one-year moratorium on hyperscale data center construction (SB232), sales tax incentives for data centers (HB1005), and restrictions on AI use in healthcare and therapy settings. The state's legislative activity is notably weighted toward consumer protection and grid stability rather than unfettered industry growth, making it one of the more cautious energy-focused environments for data center development in the region.

legislation.title: Protect residents from increased utility costs and utility shortages caused by data centers and clarify authority to regulate data centers.
legislation.summary: This bill aims to protect South Dakota residents from higher utility bills and power shortages that could result from data center electricity demand. It also clarifies which government authorities have the power to regulate data centers and their utility usage.

legislation.title: Place requirements on the use of artificial intelligence systems by health carriers in making determinations about the provision of health care services.
legislation.summary: This bill requires health insurance companies in South Dakota to follow specific rules when using AI systems to make decisions about covering or providing health care services. It aims to ensure AI-driven coverage determinations are fair, transparent, and accountable.

legislation.title: Limit costs and risks associated with electricity use by data centers and impose a moratorium.
legislation.summary: This bill places a temporary ban on new data center construction in South Dakota while addressing the financial and grid strain that large data centers place on electricity systems. It aims to protect other electricity users from higher costs and reliability risks caused by the heavy power demands of data centers.

legislation.title: Protect residents from increased utility costs and utility shortages caused by data centers, to clarify authority to regulate data centers, and to modify provisions pertaining to the purchasing of goods and services by a data center.
legislation.summary: This South Dakota bill aims to shield residents from higher utility bills and power shortages caused by data centers by giving regulators clearer authority to oversee them. It also updates rules around what goods and services data centers are allowed to purchase.

legislation.title: Provide a sales and use tax exemption for goods and services related to data center operations.
legislation.summary: This bill exempts data center equipment and services from South Dakota sales and use taxes. It is designed to attract data center businesses to the state by reducing their operating costs.

legislation.title: Allow the Public Utilities Commission to assess actual costs to data centers that are customers of public utilities.
legislation.summary: This bill would allow South Dakota's Public Utilities Commission to charge data centers the real costs they impose on the power grid, rather than standard utility rates. It aims to ensure that large data center electricity consumers pay their fair share of grid infrastructure expenses.

legislation.title: Restrict the use of artificial intelligence in therapy and psychotherapy services and to provide a penalty therefor.
legislation.summary: This bill bans the use of artificial intelligence in providing therapy and psychotherapy services in South Dakota. Violations would result in a penalty, effectively preventing AI systems from delivering mental health counseling to patients.

legislation.title: Regulate the use of chatbots by minors.
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules on how chatbots can be used by minors in South Dakota. It likely requires safeguards or restrictions to protect children interacting with AI-powered chat tools.

legislation.title: Limit nuisances caused by data centers.
legislation.summary: This bill places limits on nuisances created by data centers, such as noise and other disturbances that can affect nearby residents. It aims to regulate how data centers operate to reduce their negative impact on surrounding communities.

legislation.title: Impose a one-year moratorium on the construction or expansion of hyperscale data centers.
legislation.summary: This bill places a one-year ban on building or expanding large-scale data centers in South Dakota. During the pause, the state would have time to evaluate the impacts of hyperscale facilities before allowing new construction to proceed.

legislation.title: Create a taskforce to study the impact of artificial intelligence systems on the state.
legislation.summary: This bill establishes a taskforce to examine how artificial intelligence systems affect South Dakota. The group would study AI's impacts and likely report findings to inform future policy decisions.
